production_logs.user_query: What was our Q3 churn rate for the enterprise cohort?
production_logs.llm_output: The enterprise cohort Q3 churn rate was 14.2%, driven by client departures in the logistics sector.
production_logs.retrieval_context: Internal Report: Q3 enterprise churn stood at 4.2%. Small-to-medium business (SMB) churn was 14.2%. Logistics sector showed 2% improvement.
production_logs.groundedness_reason: Critical Hallucination: The LLM conflated SMB churn (14.2%) with Enterprise churn (4.2%) and fabricated a narrative about logistics sector departures.

production_logs.user_query: How do I reset my account password?
production_logs.llm_output: To reset your password, click on your profile icon, navigate to Security Settings, and select 'Reset Password'. Check your email for a verification link.
production_logs.retrieval_context: Knowledge Base: Password resets are handled via the login page 'Forgot Password' link or inside Account Settings > Security.
production_logs.groundedness_reason: Fully Grounded: Output aligns perfectly with the security settings documentation provided.

production_logs.user_query: Summarize our latest compliance rules for GDPR.
production_logs.llm_output: I am sorry, but I am having trouble accessing the compliance folders right now. Please try again later.

production_logs.groundedness_reason: Grounded but empty: The model did not hallucinate, but it failed to retrieve the data properly.

Scenario A: High Quality Run.context: Refund Policy: Customers can request a full refund within 14 days of purchase. Digital downloads are non-refundable once downloaded.
Scenario A: High Quality Run.query: Can I get my money back for a software ebook I downloaded 2 days ago?
Scenario A: High Quality Run.output: No, digital downloads are non-refundable once they have been downloaded, despite the standard 14-day window.
Scenario A: High Quality Run.groundedness_reason: Passed: The system correctly identified the digital download exception in the policy.

Scenario B: Severe Hallucination.context: API Limits: Free tier accounts get 1,000 requests per month. Premium accounts get 50,000 requests.

Scenario B: Severe Hallucination.output: If you hit 1,500 requests on the free tier, your account will be automatically charged $15 for an overage tier allocation.
